我諗你買Breville smart grinder 啦。如果真係好想兩用 轉黎轉去

首先你要2000 under, 又要best, 又要電。

我真係無野推到俾你，一係你學英國佬二手價收部有暗病的靚電磨番來維修，否則你存多點錢先買仲好。

手磨上面有人提左comandate，就滿足到你除電以外所有要求。

磨豆機係最重要的硬件，其他野可以cheap，磨一定要好。

replies.msg:
我懷疑樓主唔識飲咖啡豆的個性味多點，
鋪頭的確 grinder用貴的多，但blooming 要幾耐有咩效果，沖的方法唔同，水流大小等等變數就唔到你自己控制了。好似我早排飲果包藍山豆，想口味濃厚點就可以係靚磨的刻度調校已經好有分別，再來先到上面提過手法同其他變數拉上補下咁上下

我知你話唔係咖啡狂熱者而係精明消費者。
但想講你所講的窮版niche 真係未必俾到你想要的價值。

不過講咩都未聽唔入耳同去硬馬，祝你用得開心好過

我懷疑樓主唔識飲咖啡豆的個性味多點，
鋪頭的確 grinder用貴的多，但blooming 要幾耐有咩效果，沖的方法唔同，水流大小等等變數就唔到你自己控制了。好似我早排飲果包藍山豆，想口味濃厚點就可以係靚磨的刻度調校已經好有分別，再來先到上面提過手法同其他變數拉上補下咁上下

我知你話唔係咖啡狂熱者而係精明消費者。
但想講你所講的窮版niche 真係未必俾到你想要的價值。

不過講咩都未聽唔入耳同去硬馬，祝你用得開心好過

replies.msg:
中文唔係我native等我用英文覆你
Well, I really irked you havent I
I found myself on the other end of this unsubstantiated ridicule for no compelling reason, but fine ill be civilised, so hear me out.

A man's palate for coffee or in fact whatsoever requires tasting, does not change accordingly to his will to spend. You can spend a heck amount of money and never pick up any minute differences in taste, when it comes to taste, money is inmaterial. Insofar, your first point serves no purpose but to ridicule me and I find it quite defamative.

Subsequently, I also find your point on how home brewing is superior to be inapposite; I have never suggested that coffee is better at cafe im merely refuting the point of how getting the most expensive grinder is not the end of discussion, as you have neatly commented, a good cup of coffee is subjected to ratios, grind size, water temperature, etc; would those controls diminish with a cheap grinder? The reasonable answer would be no! What im getting at is coffee can be enjoyed regardless of money spent! p.s. if you have been paying attention I am more of an espresso drinker and your example on blooming really doesnt apply here.

Now, on the purpose of this post. Its incentive is to seek recommendations for a grinder within $2000 that can deliver decent grinds i.e. good consistent grind, average particle uniformity and capable of grinding fine; and grinders under 2000 do exist!. Now i do understand a better grinder gives a better result but at some point a line has to be drawn and that line differs to each their own. At the end, Im happy with the taste a 2000 grinder brings and i consider the coffee it brings an upgrade to starbucks.

The meaning of this post was never a quest to getting the best grinder money can buy, if that is what you wanted then im terribly sorry you have to look elsewhere instead of inciting a fight in my post.

With all that said, im happy to agree to disagree.

Subsequently, I also find your point on how home brewing is superior to be inapposite; I have never suggested that coffee is better at cafe im merely refuting the point of how getting the most expensive grinder is not the end of discussion, as you have neatly commented, a good cup of coffee is subjected to ratios, grind size, water temperature, etc; would those controls diminish with a cheap grinder? The reasonable answer would be no! What im getting at is coffee can be enjoyed regardless of money spent! p.s. if you have been paying attention I am more of an espresso drinker and your example on blooming really doesnt apply here.

Now, on the purpose of this post. Its incentive is to seek recommendations for a grinder within $2000 that can deliver decent grinds i.e. good consistent grind, average particle uniformity and capable of grinding fine; and grinders under 2000 do exist!. Now i do understand a better grinder gives a better result but at some point a line has to be drawn and that line differs to each their own. At the end, Im happy with the taste a 2000 grinder brings and i consider the coffee it brings an upgrade to starbucks.

The meaning of this post was never a quest to getting the best grinder money can buy, if that is what you wanted then im terribly sorry you have to look elsewhere instead of inciting a fight in my post.

With all that said, im happy to agree to disagree.

replies.msg:
First of all, look at the topic and it’s said best coffee grinder under 2000. While you just claimed that was not getting the best grinder? How the hell could I understood that the different definition for BEST between you and me. I am so sorry about that.

其實打英文真係唔會令人高級d, 文筆的優雅同人的格調自然會人體現一切。為左對英文未必太理解的其他巴絲能夠參與討論，接下來我會用番中文

你提及good consistent grind, average particle uniformity and capable of grinding fine

其實呢度大部分人都講緊2000下的「電」磨係滿足唔到你呢堆要求。除左我之外個個都係講呢樣野，其次我地都係叫你不如儲多些少買niche 仲好future proof，你的回覆係超出筆直，又話經濟無問題你喜歡的洗一皮都得，邊撚駛要存錢on99。最後再補充話已經選好左個磨同打算換刀就應該滿足你要求。

咁點解你topic唔直接改為 「sette30+270 micro setting 整espresso 有無問題？」你係度買人野，人地俾完意見你唔受都唔緊要，有主見無問題，你咪答句out budget 同手磨必定no no咪算囉，之後去買或問sette with upgrade有咩睇法咪仲好。

你最後決定都本身內定，咁你問來做咩者？show off唔係咁架

quote.msg: 咁咪出街飲，洗錢洗到半桶水返到黎效果唔好，你嗰二千咪又係掉左落鹹水海

我懷疑樓主唔識飲咖啡豆的個性味多點，
鋪頭的確 grinder用貴的多，但blooming 要幾耐有咩效果，沖的方法唔同，水流大小等等變數就唔到你自己控制了。好似我早排飲果包藍山豆，想口味濃厚點就可以係靚磨的刻度調校已經好有分別，再來先到上面提過手法同其他變數拉上補下咁上下

我知你話唔係咖啡狂熱者而係精明消費者。
但想講你所講的窮版niche 真係未必俾到你想要的價值。

不過講咩都未聽唔入耳同去硬馬，祝你用得開心好過

中文唔係我native等我用英文覆你
Well, I really irked you havent I
I found myself on the other end of this unsubstantiated ridicule for no compelling reason, but fine ill be civilised, so hear me out.

A man's palate for coffee or in fact whatsoever requires tasting, does not change accordingly to his will to spend. You can spend a heck amount of money and never pick up any minute differences in taste, when it comes to taste, money is inmaterial. Insofar, your first point serves no purpose but to ridicule me and I find it quite defamative.

Subsequently, I also find your point on how home brewing is superior to be inapposite; I have never suggested that coffee is better at cafe im merely refuting the point of how getting the most expensive grinder is not the end of discussion, as you have neatly commented, a good cup of coffee is subjected to ratios, grind size, water temperature, etc; would those controls diminish with a cheap grinder? The reasonable answer would be no! What im getting at is coffee can be enjoyed regardless of money spent! p.s. if you have been paying attention I am more of an espresso drinker and your example on blooming really doesnt apply here.

Now, on the purpose of this post. Its incentive is to seek recommendations for a grinder within $2000 that can deliver decent grinds i.e. good consistent grind, average particle uniformity and capable of grinding fine; and grinders under 2000 do exist!. Now i do understand a better grinder gives a better result but at some point a line has to be drawn and that line differs to each their own. At the end, Im happy with the taste a 2000 grinder brings and i consider the coffee it brings an upgrade to starbucks.

The meaning of this post was never a quest to getting the best grinder money can buy, if that is what you wanted then im terribly sorry you have to look elsewhere instead of inciting a fight in my post.

With all that said, im happy to agree to disagree.
